steps.title: Define the tested relationship
steps.content: Keller and colleagues tested whether perceived similarity between product categories predicts consumers' likelihood of buying a brand extension. The needed evidence must connect similarity ratings to purchase-probability changes.

steps.title: Look for positive correlation
steps.content: Choice A concerns general purchase volume. Choice B introduces brand recognition without proving the investigated relationship. Choice D says ratings varied while probability changes stayed similar, which would undermine prediction. A strong positive correlation between ratings and probability changes shows the possibility occurs. The answer is C.
